name: FFUF Web Fuzzing
description: Claude Code skill integrating the ffuf web fuzzer for penetration testing. Enables intelligent discovery of hidden directories, files, subdomains, and API endpoints with automated result analysis.
scan_summary: Security-focused skill that wraps the ffuf web fuzzer tool. Contains SKILL.md with expert ffuf guidance, ffuf_helper.py for result analysis, and wordlist resources. Python helper only reads local JSON files — no network calls in the script itself. Requires ffuf binary installed externally. MIT licensed. README includes ethics/safety warnings. No LICENSE file in repo (MIT referenced in README only).

findings_summary.notes: Legitimate penetration testing skill by security researcher jthack. The ffuf tool itself executes real HTTP requests against targets — users must have explicit authorization. Skill includes rate limiting guidance and ethics warnings. Python helper (ffuf_helper.py) is read-only result analysis with no dangerous operations. Inherent dual-use risk: same skill enables both authorized testing and unauthorized scanning. Appropriate for security professionals with proper authorization. Note: no standalone LICENSE file found despite MIT claim in README; star count 150 stored vs 117 live suggests discrepancy.
